name: Kick a Lucky Block
description: Check how much power you need for every zone in Kick a Lucky Block! Use our free calculator to predict your kick distance and find the best weights to buy. Updated for 2026.

faq.question: What is Kick a Lucky Block?
faq.answer: It is a Roblox experience by No More Flops where you kick lucky blocks, roll “brainrot” style rewards, survive a triggered tsunami, place pickups on your plot for cash, and train your legs with weights to kick farther. Official experience: https://www.roblox.com/games/89469502395769/Kick-a-Lucky-Block — community-written overview: https://roblox.fandom.com/wiki/No_More_Flops/Kick_a_Lucky_Block

faq.question: How should I use this hub and calculator?
faq.answer: Start from the hub for codes (when we can verify them) and the zone-oriented guides. Use Quick navigation to open the kick-distance calculator or the static zones list. Numbers always depend on what you type and the latest patch — replace placeholder tables in the repo when you have verified screenshots.

faq.question: Do you work with No More Flops or Roblox?
faq.answer: No. BloxCalc is an independent fan site. Always confirm rewards, prices, and zone gates inside the live game after patches.

faq.question: Why is the codes list empty?
faq.answer: We only publish codes we can verify from primary sources. After major updates, check the developer’s official Discord or X, then refresh this hub.

faq.question: Will I get banned for using this site?
faq.answer: Reading guides and doing your own math offline does not touch Roblox. Never use exploits; the game advertises anti-exploit enforcement.

guideSections.heading: Zones, power, and progression
paragraphs: New zones usually gate on leg power or similar stats so you cannot brute-force skips without training. Write down the requirement shown at each door before you grind, then compare it to your current power after each weight purchase.
When a patch renumbers gates, community spreadsheets disagree until someone re-screenshots every entrance. Prefer the in-game UI over second-hand lists.

guideSections.heading: Kick distance, weights, and lucky blocks
paragraphs: Weights exist to push your kick range up so lucky blocks and hazards are easier to reach. Treat every purchase as ROI: cost versus meters gained per kick, not just the highest tier in the shop.
Kicking lucky blocks triggers chaos (for example tsunami waves in the live experience). Plan stamina and positioning before you chain kicks for rewards.

guideSections.heading: Plot money and brainrot placements
paragraphs: Placing rolled brainrot on your plot earns passive money in the loop described by the developers. Re-check which slots pay best after economy tuning patches.
If two items look identical, open their detail panels — multipliers and rarity tags differ more often than the icon art suggests.
